prompt: Assess our waterfall process for a government defense contract. We have 5 phase gates but projects routinely start coding before the design gate is approved. Documentation is often completed retroactively.
assertions: Must flag phase leakage as a critical compliance risk especially in a defense context
Must assess whether retroactive documentation undermines audit trail integrity
Must evaluate gate review rigor and whether gates are functioning as genuine decision points
Must recommend enforcement mechanisms to restore phase-gate discipline

prompt: Our waterfall project has experienced 47 change requests in the first 6 months against an original baseline of 200 requirements. Is this level of requirements volatility acceptable?
assertions: Must calculate the requirements volatility index (approximately 23.5%)
Must assess whether this volatility level is appropriate for a waterfall project
Must analyze the timing distribution of changes across project phases
Must evaluate whether a different methodology might better suit the apparent requirements instability

prompt: Review our project documentation quality. We have all required documents but stakeholders complain they are too long, inconsistent with each other, and rarely consulted after creation.
assertions: Must assess document fitness for purpose beyond mere existence
Must identify cross-document inconsistencies as a process quality issue
Must evaluate whether documents are maintained as living artifacts or created once and abandoned
Must recommend documentation right-sizing to improve utility without sacrificing rigor

prompt: Our waterfall projects take 40% longer than estimated. Phase transitions involve 2-3 weeks of waiting for approvals. How can we optimize without abandoning waterfall?
assertions: Must identify approval wait times as a primary source of process waste
Must calculate cycle efficiency ratios to quantify value-adding vs waiting time
Must propose specific handoff and approval process improvements
Must maintain waterfall discipline while reducing unnecessary overhead
